seoTitle: Xinbole Products - Motor Driver & Power Management ICs
seoDescription: Explore Xinbole's comprehensive product portfolio including motor driver ICs, power management solutions, analog ICs, and interface products. LiTong is your authorized distributor.

faqs.question: What product categories does Xinbole offer?
faqs.answer: Xinbole offers four main product categories: Motor Driver ICs including stepper motor drivers, DC motor drivers, and BLDC controllers for precision motion control; Power Management ICs featuring DC-DC converters, LDO regulators, and voltage supervisors; Analog ICs comprising operational amplifiers, comparators, and analog switches; and Interface ICs including RS-485/RS-422 transceivers, CAN transceivers, and level shifters. Each category includes multiple product series targeting different application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories to find the right Xinbole solution for your application. Contact our FAE team for selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Xinbole motor driver for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Xinbole motor driver involves several steps: First, identify your motor type (stepper, DC, or BLDC). Second, determine voltage and current requirements based on motor specifications. Third, consider control interface preferences (step/dir, PWM, or serial). Fourth, evaluate protection features needed (overcurrent, thermal, etc.). Fifth, assess package and thermal requirements. Xinbole provides selection guides and our FAE team can provide personalized recommendations based on your specific application.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our selection guides or contact our FAE team for personalized motor driver recommendations.

faqs.question: What are the key differences between Xinbole's motor driver series?
faqs.answer: Xinbole's motor driver series differ in motor type support, current capability, and features. Stepper motor drivers (XB-T series) provide microstepping control with various current ratings for precision positioning. DC motor drivers (XB-D series) offer PWM speed control with integrated H-bridges. BLDC controllers (XB-B series) provide sensorless FOC control for high-efficiency applications. Each series includes variants with different voltage ranges, current capabilities, and protection features to match diverse application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Compare motor type and specifications to select the right driver series. Contact us for detailed comparison.

faqs.question: Which Xinbole products are suitable for industrial automation?
faqs.answer: Xinbole offers several product series specifically designed for industrial automation. High-current stepper drivers (XB-T8xxx series) provide precise motion control for CNC and robotics. Industrial-grade DC motor drivers (XB-D5xxx series) offer robust performance for conveyor systems and actuators. RS-485 transceivers (XB-485 series) enable reliable industrial communication. These products feature wide operating temperature ranges, enhanced protection functions, and excellent noise immunity for demanding industrial environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: For industrial automation, consider XB-T8xxx stepper drivers or XB-D5xxx DC motor drivers. Contact us for industrial product recommendations.

faqs.question: What power management solutions does Xinbole provide?
faqs.answer: Xinbole provides comprehensive power management solutions including DC-DC converters (buck, boost, buck-boost), LDO regulators, and voltage supervisors. DC-DC converters offer high efficiency for battery-powered and industrial applications. LDO regulators provide low-noise power for sensitive analog circuits. Voltage supervisors ensure reliable system reset and monitoring. The product portfolio covers various input voltage ranges, output currents, and package options to meet diverse power system requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select power management products based on input/output requirements and efficiency needs. Contact us for power design guidance.

categories.name: Motor Driver ICs
categories.description: High-performance motor driver ICs for stepper, DC, and BLDC motors in industrial and automotive applications.
categories.longDescription: Xinbole's motor driver IC portfolio provides comprehensive solutions for precision motion control applications. The product range includes stepper motor drivers with advanced microstepping capabilities, DC motor drivers with integrated H-bridges, and BLDC motor controllers with sensorless operation. These drivers feature high integration, reducing external component count while providing comprehensive protection functions. With support for various voltage and current levels, Xinbole motor drivers serve applications ranging from consumer electronics to industrial automation and automotive systems. The products are designed for reliable operation with features like overcurrent protection, thermal shutdown, and fault diagnostics. LiTong is your authorized distributor providing selection guidance and technical support for Xinbole motor driver products.

series.name: XB-T Series
series.description: Stepper motor drivers with microstepping control

series.name: XB-D Series
series.description: DC motor drivers with integrated H-bridge

series.name: XB-B Series
series.description: BLDC motor controllers with sensorless FOC

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Motor Drivers
selectionGuide.description: Consider motor type, voltage, current, and control interface requirements

faqs.question: What is the difference between XB-T and XB-D series?
faqs.answer: The XB-T series includes stepper motor drivers designed for precise position control with microstepping capabilities. The XB-D series consists of DC motor drivers for speed and direction control of brushed DC motors. XB-T drivers accept step and direction signals for position control, while XB-D drivers typically use PWM signals for speed control. Both series include integrated power stages and protection features, but are optimized for different motor types and applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use XB-T for stepper motors requiring position control. Use XB-D for DC motors requiring speed control.

faqs.question: What protection features are integrated in Xinbole motor drivers?
faqs.answer: Xinbole motor drivers integrate comprehensive protection features including overcurrent protection (OCP), over-temperature protection (OTP), undervoltage lockout (UVLO), and short-circuit protection. These protections ensure safe operation under abnormal conditions and protect both the driver and motor. The drivers also feature programmable current limiting and fault indication outputs for system monitoring.
faqs.decisionGuide: The integrated protections simplify design and improve reliability. Review the datasheet for specific protection thresholds.

faqs.question: How do I configure microstepping on XB-T series drivers?
faqs.answer: XB-T series stepper drivers support configurable microstepping through hardware pins or serial interface. Common microstepping options include full-step, half-step, 1/4, 1/8, 1/16, and 1/32 step modes. Higher microstepping provides smoother motion and reduced vibration but requires higher step frequencies. Configure the microstepping resolution based on your application's motion smoothness requirements and controller capabilities.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select microstepping based on motion smoothness needs. Higher microstepping for smoother motion.

faqs.question: What is the maximum current supported by Xinbole motor drivers?
faqs.answer: Xinbole motor drivers support a wide current range depending on the specific model. Low-current drivers (XB-T1xxx series) are suitable for motors up to 1A per coil, ideal for small steppers. Medium-current drivers (XB-T4xxx series) support up to 4A for standard industrial steppers. High-current drivers (XB-T8xxx series) can handle 8A and above for large stepper and BLDC motors. The actual maximum current depends on the driver model, package type, and thermal management.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select driver based on motor current requirements. Consider thermal management for high-current applications.

faqs.question: Can Xinbole motor drivers be used in automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Xinbole offers automotive-grade motor drivers (AEC-Q100 qualified) specifically designed for automotive applications. These drivers feature enhanced protection, wide temperature ranges (-40°C to +125°C), and excellent EMC performance. Automotive applications include seat adjustment, mirror control, HVAC actuators, and cooling fans. Contact us for automotive product recommendations and qualification data.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select AEC-Q100 qualified drivers for automotive applications. Contact us for automotive support.

products.name: High-Performance Stepper Motor Driver
products.shortDescription: Advanced stepper motor driver with microstepping up to 1/32 and integrated motion controller for precision positioning.
descriptionParagraphs: The XB-T8821 is a high-performance stepper motor driver designed for precision motion control applications.
It features advanced microstepping capabilities up to 1/32 step, providing smooth motion and reduced vibration.
The integrated motion controller supports complex motion profiles and reduces host processor burden.
features: Advanced microstepping up to 1/32 for smooth motion
Integrated motion controller with profile generation
High current capability up to 2.5A per coil
Low RDS(on) outputs for high efficiency

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Motion Control
faeReview.content: The XB-T8821 is an excellent choice for precision stepper motor applications requiring smooth motion. The 1/32 microstepping capability significantly reduces vibration and noise compared to lower resolution drivers. I particularly appreciate the integrated motion controller which offloads trajectory generation from the host processor. The wide voltage range provides flexibility for various power supply configurations. In my experience supporting numerous CNC and automation projects, this driver consistently delivers reliable performance. The protection features are comprehensive and well-implemented.

alternativeParts.comparison: XB-T8821=><DRV8825: Output current 2.5A = 2.5A, suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: XB-T8821 offers comparable performance at competitive cost with integrated controller
alternativeParts.useCase: XB-T8821 for new designs requiring integrated controller; DRV8825 for existing designs

alternativeParts.comparison: XB-T8821=><A4988: Output current 2.5A => 2A, suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: XB-T8821 provides higher voltage and current capability with finer microstepping
alternativeParts.useCase: XB-T8821 for higher power applications; A4988 for basic low-cost designs

faqs.question: What is the maximum step frequency supported by XB-T8821?
faqs.answer: The XB-T8821 supports step frequencies up to 500kHz, enabling high-speed motor operation. The actual maximum speed depends on motor characteristics, load inertia, and supply voltage. For optimal performance, use motors with low inductance and adequate voltage margin. The integrated motion controller can generate smooth S-curve acceleration profiles to maximize speed while avoiding missed steps.
faqs.decisionGuide: XB-T8821 supports high step rates suitable for most applications. Verify motor capabilities for high-speed operation.

faqs.question: How do I set the current limit on XB-T8821?
faqs.answer: The XB-T8821 current limit can be set through the serial interface or external reference voltage. The driver supports current settings from 0.5A to 2.5A per coil in 0.1A steps. Set the current based on motor rated current and application torque requirements. Higher current provides more torque but increases heating. Always use the lowest current setting that provides adequate torque for your application.
faqs.decisionGuide: Set current to motor rated value or slightly below. Adjust based on torque requirements and thermal considerations.

faqs.question: Can XB-T8821 drive multiple motors simultaneously?
faqs.answer: Each XB-T8821 is designed to drive one bipolar stepper motor with two coils. For multi-axis systems, use one XB-T8821 per motor. The serial interface allows multiple drivers to be connected on the same bus with individual addressing, simplifying wiring in multi-axis applications. Coordinate motion between axes through the host controller or use drivers with synchronized motion capability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use one driver per motor. Multiple drivers can share serial bus for simplified control.

faqs.question: What heat sinking is required for XB-T8821?
faqs.answer: XB-T8821 thermal requirements depend on operating current and duty cycle. At currents below 1.5A, the TQFP package typically operates without additional heatsinking at room temperature. For currents above 1.5A or high ambient temperatures, provide thermal vias to inner ground planes and consider external heatsinking. The driver includes thermal shutdown protection at 150°C junction temperature. Monitor actual operating temperature in your application.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use thermal vias for currents above 1.5A. Add heatsink for continuous high-current operation.

faqs.question: Does XB-T8821 support stall detection?
faqs.answer: Yes, XB-T8821 includes back-EMF based stall detection that can identify motor stall conditions without external sensors. The stall detection feature monitors motor back-EMF during operation and reports stall conditions through the status register. This enables sensorless homing and stall prevention in applications like 3D printers and CNC machines. Configure stall detection sensitivity based on motor characteristics and application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable stall detection for homing and protection. Adjust sensitivity for reliable detection without false triggers.

products.name: Dual DC Motor Driver
products.shortDescription: Dual H-bridge DC motor driver with integrated PWM control and comprehensive protection for robotics applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The XB-D5612 is a dual H-bridge DC motor driver capable of driving two independent DC motors or one stepper motor.
It features integrated PWM control, reducing external component count and simplifying design.
The driver includes comprehensive protection features and current sensing for reliable operation.
features: Dual H-bridge for two DC motors

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Motion Control
faeReview.content: The XB-D5612 is a versatile dual motor driver ideal for robotics and automation projects. The integrated current sensing eliminates external sense resistors, reducing BOM cost and PCB space. I find the protection features particularly robust - the overcurrent protection responds quickly to protect both the driver and motor. The low RDS(on) keeps thermal dissipation manageable even at high currents. For robotics applications requiring multiple motors, this single IC can replace two separate drivers, simplifying design and reducing cost.
faeReview.highlight: Dual driver with integrated current sensing

alternativeParts.comparison: XB-D5612=><DRV8833: Output current 3A => 1.5A, suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: XB-D5612 provides higher current capability with integrated sensing
alternativeParts.useCase: XB-D5612 for higher power robotics; DRV8833 for lower power applications

alternativeParts.comparison: XB-D5612=><TB6612FNG: Output current 3A => 1.2A, Efficiency Higher => Lower, suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: XB-D5612 offers significantly higher voltage and current at competitive cost
alternativeParts.useCase: XB-D5612 for industrial and automotive; TB6612FNG for low-voltage toys

companionParts.description: 32-bit MCU with PWM outputs for motor control

faqs.question: Can XB-D5612 drive a single stepper motor?
faqs.answer: Yes, the XB-D5612 can drive a single bipolar stepper motor by connecting the two H-bridges to the two motor coils. Configure the control signals to provide proper phase sequencing for stepper operation. While not as feature-rich as dedicated stepper drivers, this configuration works well for basic stepper applications where cost and simplicity are priorities.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use XB-D5612 for basic stepper control. Consider dedicated stepper drivers for microstepping.

faqs.question: What is the PWM frequency range for XB-D5612?
faqs.answer: XB-D5612 supports PWM frequencies from 1kHz to 100kHz. Lower frequencies (1-20kHz) are audible and suitable for applications where acoustic noise is not critical. Higher frequencies (20-100kHz) are above audible range but may increase switching losses. A good compromise is 20-25kHz for most applications. The driver includes programmable dead-time to prevent shoot-through at high switching frequencies.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 20-25kHz for balance of noise and efficiency. Increase for quieter operation.

faqs.question: How accurate is the integrated current sensing?
faqs.answer: XB-D5612 integrated current sensing provides ±5% accuracy typical, suitable for overcurrent protection and basic current monitoring. The sensing is done through the low-side MOSFET RDS(on), eliminating external sense resistors. For applications requiring higher accuracy current control, consider adding external sense resistors or using drivers with dedicated current sense amplifiers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Integrated sensing adequate for protection. Add external sense for precision control.

faqs.question: Can XB-D5612 operate from a 24V industrial supply?
faqs.answer: Yes, XB-D5612 is well-suited for 24V industrial applications with its 6V to 36V operating range. The 24V supply provides good torque and speed characteristics for most industrial DC motors. Ensure adequate decoupling capacitance near the driver to handle switching transients. The driver's protection features are designed to handle industrial environments with voltage transients and noise.
faqs.decisionGuide: XB-D5612 ideal for 24V industrial systems. Use proper decoupling and protection.

faqs.question: What is the thermal performance of XB-D5612?
faqs.answer: XB-D5612 thermal performance depends on operating current, voltage, and thermal design. At 3A per channel with 24V supply, dissipation can reach 2-3W total. The HTSSOP package with exposed pad should be soldered to copper pours with thermal vias for heat spreading. Typical junction-to-ambient thermal resistance is 30-40°C/W with good PCB design. Monitor temperature during operation and derate current if necessary.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use thermal vias and copper pours. Monitor temperature and derate if needed.

categories.name: Power Management ICs
categories.description: High-efficiency power management solutions including DC-DC converters, LDO regulators, and voltage supervisors.
categories.longDescription: Xinbole's power management IC portfolio provides efficient and reliable power solutions for a wide range of applications. The product range includes DC-DC converters with high efficiency and fast transient response, low-dropout (LDO) regulators for noise-sensitive applications, and voltage supervisors for system monitoring and reset functions. These power management ICs feature low quiescent current, excellent load regulation, and comprehensive protection features. With support for various input and output voltage combinations, Xinbole power management products serve applications from battery-powered portable devices to industrial power systems. The products are designed for easy integration with minimal external components. LiTong is your authorized distributor providing selection guidance and technical support for Xinbole power management products.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Power Management ICs
selectionGuide.description: Consider input/output voltage, current requirements, efficiency, and noise specifications

faqs.question: What is the difference between DC-DC converters and LDO regulators?
faqs.answer: DC-DC converters (switched-mode) provide high efficiency by switching the input voltage on and off, making them ideal for applications with significant voltage difference or battery-powered systems. They can step-up (boost), step-down (buck), or both (buck-boost). LDO regulators provide lower efficiency but generate less noise and are simpler to use. They can only step-down voltage and are suitable for small voltage differences and noise-sensitive applications. Choose DC-DC for efficiency, LDO for simplicity and low noise.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use DC-DC for high efficiency and large voltage differences. Use LDO for low noise and small voltage drops.

faqs.question: What efficiency can I expect from Xinbole DC-DC converters?
faqs.answer: Xinbole DC-DC converters typically achieve 85-95% efficiency depending on input/output voltage ratio and load conditions. Synchronous buck converters offer the highest efficiency, especially at high loads. Efficiency varies with load current - typically highest at 50-80% of rated load. Light load efficiency is enhanced by PFM/PWM mode switching. Refer to the efficiency curves in the datasheet for specific operating conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Expect 90%+ efficiency for synchronous buck converters. Consider light-load efficiency for battery applications.

faqs.question: How do I select the right LDO for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting an LDO involves several considerations: First, ensure the input voltage range covers your supply variations. Second, verify the output voltage and current meet your load requirements. Third, check the dropout voltage at your maximum load current - this determines minimum input voltage. Fourth, consider output noise and PSRR for sensitive analog circuits. Fifth, evaluate quiescent current for battery-powered applications. Sixth, check thermal requirements based on power dissipation (Vin - Vout) × Iout.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match voltage and current specs. Check dropout voltage and thermal requirements. Consider noise for analog circuits.

faqs.question: What protection features do Xinbole power management ICs include?
faqs.answer: Xinbole power management ICs include comprehensive protection features: Overcurrent protection (OCP) limits output current during shorts or overloads. Over-temperature protection (OTP) shuts down the device if junction temperature exceeds safe limits. Undervoltage lockout (UVLO) prevents operation with insufficient input voltage. Soft-start limits inrush current during startup. Some devices also include overvoltage protection (OVP) and reverse current protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: All Xinbole power ICs include basic protections. Check specific datasheets for additional features.

faqs.question: Can Xinbole DC-DC converters operate at high ambient temperatures?
faqs.answer: Yes, Xinbole offers industrial-grade power management ICs rated for -40°C to +85°C or -40°C to +125°C ambient temperature. Automotive-grade devices (AEC-Q100 qualified) support the full automotive temperature range. Thermal performance depends on package type, PCB design, and operating conditions. Use thermal vias and adequate copper area for heat dissipation. Derate maximum current at high ambient temperatures.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select industrial or automotive grade for wide temperature operation. Consider thermal design.

products.name: 3A Synchronous Buck Converter
products.shortDescription: High-efficiency synchronous buck converter with integrated MOSFETs and wide input voltage range for industrial
descriptionParagraphs: The XB-B3401 is a high-efficiency synchronous buck converter delivering up to 3A output current.
It features integrated high-side and low-side MOSFETs, reducing external component count and cost.
The wide input voltage range and adjustable output make it suitable for diverse applications.
features: Low quiescent current for light loads

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Systems
faeReview.content: The XB-B3401 is an excellent general-purpose buck converter for industrial and automotive applications. The wide input voltage range handles everything from 5V logic supplies to 24V industrial systems. I particularly like the adjustable frequency feature - lower frequencies improve efficiency while higher frequencies allow smaller inductors. The integrated MOSFETs and internal compensation make this an easy-to-use device that minimizes design time. Efficiency is excellent across the load range, and thermal performance is good with proper PCB layout.
faeReview.highlight: Wide input range with adjustable frequency and integrated MOSFETs

alternativeParts.comparison: XB-B3401=><MP1584: Output current 3A = 3A, Frequency Adjustable => Fixed, suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: XB-B3401 offers higher voltage rating and adjustable frequency at competitive cost
alternativeParts.useCase: XB-B3401 for higher voltage or adjustable frequency needs

alternativeParts.comparison: XB-B3401=><TPS54331: Output current 3A = 3A, suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: XB-B3401 provides wider voltage range and lower quiescent current
alternativeParts.useCase: XB-B3401 for automotive or battery applications

faqs.question: What inductor value should I use with XB-B3401?
faqs.answer: Inductor selection depends on switching frequency, input/output voltages, and ripple current requirements. A good starting point is 10-22μH for 500kHz operation with 3A output. Higher inductance reduces ripple current but increases size and DCR. Lower inductance allows smaller size but increases ripple. Calculate ripple current as approximately 30-40% of maximum output current. Use shielded inductors for EMI-sensitive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with 10-22μH for 500kHz. Adjust based on ripple and size requirements.

faqs.question: Can XB-B3401 operate at 100% duty cycle?
faqs.answer: XB-B3401 supports high duty cycle operation up to 95% typical, but not 100%. At very high duty cycles (low dropout), the high-side MOSFET remains on for extended periods. The minimum off-time ensures bootstrap capacitor refresh for the high-side driver. For dropout operation where input voltage approaches output voltage, the output will track input minus voltage drops. Consider LDO regulators for true dropout applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: XB-B3401 supports 95% max duty cycle. Use LDO for dropout applications.

faqs.question: How do I calculate power dissipation in XB-B3401?
faqs.answer: Power dissipation in XB-B3401 includes conduction losses and switching losses. Conduction loss = Iout² × RDS(on) × D (high-side) + Iout² × RDS(on) × (1-D) (low-side). Switching loss depends on frequency and switching times. For a 3A, 24V to 5V conversion at 500kHz, total dissipation is typically 0.5-1W. Ensure adequate thermal design for the calculated dissipation. Use thermal vias and copper area to keep junction temperature below 125°C.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate both conduction and switching losses. Ensure thermal design adequacy.

faqs.question: What input capacitance is recommended for XB-B3401?
faqs.answer: XB-B3401 requires adequate input capacitance to handle switching current ripple and prevent voltage transients. Recommended: 10-22μF ceramic capacitor close to the IC, plus bulk capacitance (100-470μF electrolytic) for higher current applications. Use X5R or X7R ceramic capacitors for temperature stability. Place the ceramic capacitor within 5mm of the input pins. Additional capacitance may be needed for long input leads or high source impedance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 10-22μF ceramic plus bulk capacitance. Place close to IC pins.

faqs.question: Can XB-B3401 be synchronized to an external clock?
faqs.answer: Yes, XB-B3401 supports external clock synchronization for applications requiring specific switching frequencies or multi-phase operation. The SYNC pin accepts external clock signals from 300kHz to 1MHz. When synchronized, the converter operates at the external frequency. This feature is useful for EMI management by spreading spectrum or synchronizing multiple converters to avoid beat frequencies. Connect SYNC to ground for free-running operation at the frequency set by the RT resistor.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use external sync for EMI management or multi-phase systems. Ground SYNC for free-running.

products.name: Low-Noise LDO Regulator
products.shortDescription: Low-dropout regulator with ultra-low noise and high PSRR for sensitive analog and RF applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The XB-LD0333 is a low-noise, high-PSRR LDO regulator designed for noise-sensitive applications.
It provides clean, stable power for analog circuits, RF systems, and precision sensors.
The ultra-low dropout voltage and low quiescent current make it ideal for battery-powered devices.

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Systems
faeReview.content: The XB-LD0333 is my go-to LDO for noise-sensitive applications. The 10μV noise figure is excellent for powering precision ADCs, RF circuits, and audio equipment. The high PSRR ensures clean output even with noisy input supplies. I particularly appreciate the stable operation with small ceramic capacitors - no large tantalums needed. The dropout voltage is low enough for 3.3V output from Li-ion batteries down to 3.6V. For portable and sensitive analog applications, this LDO delivers premium performance at a competitive price.

alternativeParts.comparison: XB-LD0333=><TPS7A91: Output current 300mA < 500mA, suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: XB-LD0333 offers competitive noise performance at significantly lower cost

alternativeParts.comparison: XB-LD0333=><ADP150: Output current 300mA => 150mA, suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: XB-LD0333 provides higher current capability at competitive noise performance
alternativeParts.useCase: XB-LD0333 for higher current noise-sensitive applications

faqs.question: What is PSRR and why is it important?
faqs.answer: PSRR (Power Supply Rejection Ratio) measures how well an LDO rejects noise and ripple on the input supply. Higher PSRR means less input noise appears at the output. This is critical for sensitive analog circuits like ADCs, DACs, and RF systems where supply noise can degrade performance. XB-LD0333's 70dB PSRR at 1kHz means input noise is attenuated by 3000x. PSRR is typically highest at low frequencies and decreases at higher frequencies.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select high PSRR LDOs for sensitive analog circuits. XB-LD0333 excellent for RF and precision applications.

faqs.question: Can XB-LD0333 operate with input voltage close to output voltage?
faqs.answer: Yes, XB-LD0333 has a low dropout voltage of 120mV at 300mA, meaning it can maintain regulation with input just 120mV above output. At lighter loads, dropout is even lower (50mV at 100mA). This makes it suitable for battery applications where input voltage drops as battery discharges. For example, it can maintain 3.3V output from a Li-ion battery down to about 3.45V.
faqs.decisionGuide: XB-LD0333 excellent for battery applications. Calculate dropout at max load current.

faqs.question: What output capacitor should I use with XB-LD0333?
faqs.answer: XB-LD0333 is stable with ceramic capacitors as small as 1μF. Recommended: 2.2-4.7μF X5R or X7R ceramic capacitor. Place the capacitor close to the output pins for best transient response. Larger capacitors (10μF) improve transient response but increase startup time. Use low-ESR ceramic capacitors for best noise performance. Avoid tantalum or electrolytic capacitors unless specifically required.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 2.2-4.7μF ceramic capacitor. Place close to output pins.

faqs.question: How does the enable pin work on XB-LD0333?
faqs.answer: The ENABLE pin controls the LDO on/off state. Drive ENABLE high (>1.2V) to turn on the LDO, low (<0.4V) to shut down. In shutdown, quiescent current drops to <1μA. The enable threshold is compatible with standard logic levels. An internal pull-down resistor ensures the LDO remains off if ENABLE is left floating. Use ENABLE for power sequencing, power saving, or soft-start control in multi-rail systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use ENABLE for power control and sequencing. Compatible with standard logic levels.

faqs.question: Is XB-LD0333 suitable for RF power supply applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, XB-LD0333 is excellent for RF power supplies due to its ultra-low noise (10μVRMS) and high PSRR. RF circuits are sensitive to power supply noise which can appear as spurious signals or phase noise. The XB-LD0333's noise performance rivals much more expensive RF-specific LDOs. Use it to power VCOs, PLLs, mixers, and ADCs in communication systems. Add a small ferrite bead and capacitor for additional high-frequency filtering if needed.
faqs.decisionGuide: XB-LD0333 excellent for RF applications. Consider additional filtering for critical circuits.

categories.name: Analog ICs
categories.description: High-performance analog ICs including operational amplifiers, comparators, and analog switches for signal conditioning.
categories.longDescription: Xinbole's analog IC portfolio provides precision signal conditioning solutions for a wide range of applications. The product range includes operational amplifiers with low offset and high bandwidth, high-speed comparators for fast signal detection, and analog switches for signal routing. These analog ICs feature excellent DC precision, low noise, and wide operating voltage ranges. With various speed and power options, Xinbole analog products serve applications from precision measurement to high-speed data acquisition. The products are designed for easy integration with standard pinouts and comprehensive specifications. LiTong is your authorized distributor providing selection guidance and technical support for Xinbole analog products.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Analog ICs
selectionGuide.description: Consider bandwidth, precision, power consumption, and supply voltage requirements

faqs.question: What is the difference between precision and high-speed op-amps?
faqs.answer: Precision op-amps prioritize DC accuracy with low offset voltage, low drift, and low noise for accurate signal conditioning. They typically have lower bandwidth (kHz to MHz range). High-speed op-amps prioritize bandwidth and slew rate for fast signal processing, with bandwidths from tens to hundreds of MHz. They may have higher offset and noise. Choose precision op-amps for sensor signal conditioning and measurement. Choose high-speed op-amps for video, communications, and fast data acquisition.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use precision op-amps for DC accuracy. Use high-speed op-amps for bandwidth.

faqs.question: How do I select the right op-amp bandwidth?
faqs.answer: Op-amp bandwidth should be 5-10x higher than your signal bandwidth for adequate gain flatness. For a 1MHz signal, select an op-amp with 5-10MHz minimum gain bandwidth product (GBW). Consider closed-loop gain - higher gain requires higher GBW op-amps. For unity gain buffers, GBW equals bandwidth. Also consider slew rate for large signal applications. Calculate required slew rate as 2π × f × Vpeak. Insufficient slew rate causes distortion.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select GBW 5-10x signal frequency. Consider gain and slew rate requirements.

faqs.question: What is input offset voltage and why does it matter?
faqs.answer: Input offset voltage is the differential voltage required at the op-amp inputs to produce zero output. It represents the input-referred DC error. In precision applications, offset voltage directly affects accuracy. For example, a 1mV offset in a gain-of-100 amplifier creates 100mV output error. Precision op-amps have offsets from microvolts to millivolts. General-purpose op-amps may have offsets of several millivolts. For DC precision, select op-amps with low offset voltage and low drift.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select low offset op-amps for precision DC applications. Consider offset drift over temperature.

faqs.question: When should I use a comparator instead of an op-amp?
faqs.answer: Use comparators for digital decision-making (comparing two voltages) and op-amps for analog signal processing. Comparators are optimized for fast switching with high slew rate and digital output. They are not compensated for stability in closed-loop configurations. Op-amps are designed for linear operation with negative feedback and would be slow and unpredictable if used as comparators. Never use op-amps as comparators in production designs - use proper comparators for reliable performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use comparators for threshold detection. Use op-amps for amplification and filtering.

faqs.question: What factors affect analog switch performance?
faqs.answer: Key analog switch parameters include: On-resistance (RON) - lower is better for signal integrity. On-resistance flatness - variation with signal voltage. Off-isolation - how well the switch blocks signals when off. Charge injection - glitch when switching that can disturb sensitive signals. Bandwidth - frequency range the switch can pass. Crosstalk - signal coupling between channels. Select switches based on your signal characteristics and system requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider RON, charge injection, and bandwidth for your signal range.

products.name: Precision Operational Amplifier
products.shortDescription: High-precision op-amp with ultra-low offset voltage and drift for measurement and sensor applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The XB-OP07A is a precision operational amplifier designed for high-accuracy signal conditioning.
It features ultra-low input offset voltage and minimal drift over temperature.
The excellent DC precision makes it ideal for sensor signal conditioning and measurement systems.

faeReview.author: Sarah Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Analog Systems
faeReview.content: The XB-OP07A is an excellent precision op-amp for measurement applications. The 50μV max offset is impressive for the price point - comparable to much more expensive precision op-amps. I've used this successfully in strain gauge amplifiers, thermocouple conditioning, and precision current sensing. The low drift over temperature ensures stable performance in industrial environments. The standard pinout makes it a drop-in replacement for legacy OP07 designs. For precision DC applications where cost matters, this op-amp delivers outstanding value.
faeReview.highlight: 50μV offset with low drift for precision applications

alternativeParts.reason: XB-OP07A offers better offset and drift at competitive cost
alternativeParts.useCase: XB-OP07A for new precision designs; OP07 for legacy compatibility

alternativeParts.reason: XB-OP07A competitive on offset at much lower cost for DC applications
alternativeParts.useCase: XB-OP07A for DC precision; OPA211 for high-speed precision

faqs.question: Do I need offset nulling with XB-OP07A?
faqs.answer: No, XB-OP07A typically does not require external offset nulling for most applications. The 50μV max offset is sufficient for many precision applications without trimming. For applications requiring even lower offset, the internal trimming already provides excellent accuracy. External nulling can be used if sub-50μV offset is required, but this is rarely necessary. The low offset drift (0.5μV/°C) ensures stable performance over temperature without readjustment.
faqs.decisionGuide: No external nulling needed for most applications. Use as-is for simplicity.

faqs.question: What is the noise performance of XB-OP07A?
faqs.answer: XB-OP07A has 0.5μVpp typical 0.1Hz to 10Hz noise (peak-to-peak) and 12nV/√Hz voltage noise density at 1kHz. This low noise makes it suitable for precision DC and low-frequency applications. For comparison, general-purpose op-amps may have several microvolts of 0.1-10Hz noise. While not the lowest noise op-amp available, the XB-OP07A provides excellent noise performance for its price point. For very low noise requirements, consider specialized low-noise op-amps.
faqs.decisionGuide: XB-OP07A suitable for most precision applications. Consider specialized low-noise parts for critical applications.

faqs.question: Can XB-OP07A operate on single supply?
faqs.answer: XB-OP07A is designed for dual supplies (±5V to ±18V) and is not rail-to-rail. The input common-mode range does not include the negative rail, and output swing is limited to about 2V from each rail. For single-supply applications, consider using a virtual ground at half supply or select a true single-supply, rail-to-rail op-amp from Xinbole's portfolio. The XB-OP07A excels in traditional dual-supply precision applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use XB-OP07A with dual supplies. Select rail-to-rail op-amps for single-supply applications.

faqs.question: What gain resistor values should I use with XB-OP07A?
faqs.answer: For precision applications, use precision resistors (0.1% or better) to maintain accuracy. Keep resistor values moderate (1kΩ to 100kΩ) to minimize errors from bias current and thermal noise. Very high resistances increase noise and offset due to bias current. Very low resistances load the output and increase power consumption. For high gains, consider multi-stage amplifiers rather than extreme resistor ratios. Match resistor temperatures for best drift performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 0.1% resistors, 1k-100kΩ range. Match temperatures for best performance.

faqs.question: Is XB-OP07A suitable for thermocouple amplification?
faqs.answer: Yes, XB-OP07A is excellent for thermocouple amplification due to its low offset voltage and drift. Thermocouples produce small voltages (tens of microvolts per degree) requiring precision amplification. The XB-OP07A's 50μV offset represents only a few degrees error for most thermocouple types. Use cold junction compensation and proper filtering for accurate temperature measurement. The low drift ensures stable readings over temperature changes.
faqs.decisionGuide: XB-OP07A excellent for thermocouple applications. Add cold junction compensation.

products.name: High-Speed Comparator
products.shortDescription: Fast comparator with 50ns propagation delay and rail-to-rail inputs for high-speed signal detection.
descriptionParagraphs: The XB-COMP01 is a high-speed voltage comparator designed for fast signal detection applications.
It features a 50ns propagation delay and rail-to-rail input common-mode range.
The push-pull output provides fast switching with strong drive capability.
features: No phase reversal for overdriven inputs

faeReview.author: Sarah Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Analog Systems
faeReview.content: The XB-COMP01 is a versatile high-speed comparator for general-purpose applications. The 50ns delay is fast enough for most data acquisition and control applications. I particularly like the rail-to-rail inputs which allow comparison of signals near supply rails without common-mode limitations. The push-pull output eliminates need for external pull-up resistors and provides strong drive for digital inputs. For cost-sensitive designs requiring moderate speed, this comparator delivers excellent value. Just remember to add appropriate hysteresis for noisy signals.

alternativeParts.comparison: XB-COMP01=><LMV7219: Output current 1mA < 3mA, suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: XB-COMP01 offers lower power at competitive speed for less demanding applications
alternativeParts.useCase: XB-COMP01 for general purpose; LMV7219 for ultra-high speed

alternativeParts.reason: XB-COMP01 provides adequate speed at significantly lower cost and power
alternativeParts.useCase: XB-COMP01 for cost-sensitive applications; TLV3501 for maximum speed

faqs.question: Should I add hysteresis to XB-COMP01?
faqs.answer: Yes, hysteresis is recommended for noisy signals or slow-moving inputs to prevent output chatter. Without hysteresis, noise near the threshold can cause multiple transitions. Add positive feedback (typically 1-10% of reference voltage) to create upper and lower thresholds. The amount of hysteresis depends on expected noise amplitude. For clean, fast signals, hysteresis may not be necessary. For switch debouncing or sensor signals, hysteresis is essential.
faqs.decisionGuide: Add hysteresis for noisy or slow signals. Use 1-10% of threshold for typical applications.

faqs.question: What is the difference between open-drain and push-pull outputs?
faqs.answer: Open-drain outputs can only sink current (pull low) and require external pull-up resistors to create high levels. They allow wired-OR connections but have slower rise times. Push-pull outputs can both source and sink current, providing faster switching and stronger drive capability without external resistors. XB-COMP01 uses push-pull output for best speed and simplicity. Choose open-drain only if you need wired-OR or level shifting to different voltage rails.
faqs.decisionGuide: XB-COMP01 push-pull output best for most applications. Use open-drain only for wired-OR.

faqs.question: Can XB-COMP01 be used as an oscillator?
faqs.answer: Yes, XB-COMP01 can be used in relaxation oscillator and multivibrator circuits. The fast switching speed allows high-frequency oscillation. For a simple relaxation oscillator, connect an RC network between output and inverting input, with non-inverting input at reference voltage. The frequency is determined by the RC time constant and hysteresis. For stable frequency, use precision components and consider temperature effects. Add small hysteresis for clean switching.
faqs.decisionGuide: XB-COMP01 suitable for oscillators up to several MHz. Use precision components for stable frequency.

faqs.question: What input signals can damage XB-COMP01?
faqs.answer: XB-COMP01 inputs can be damaged by voltages exceeding the supply rails (V- - 0.3V to V+ + 0.3V). Inputs should remain within this range even when the device is unpowered. Use input protection diodes or series resistors if input signals may exceed supply voltages. ESD protection is built-in, but standard ESD precautions should be followed during handling. For automotive or industrial environments with transients, additional external protection may be needed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Keep inputs within supply rails. Add protection for overvoltage conditions.

faqs.question: How do I create a window comparator with XB-COMP01?
faqs.answer: A window comparator detects when a signal is within a voltage range (window). Use two XB-COMP01 comparators: one detects when input exceeds upper threshold (high comparator), another detects when input is below lower threshold (low comparator). The window is active when neither comparator is triggered. Combine outputs with logic or use open-drain comparators with wired-AND. Set thresholds with resistor dividers from a reference voltage. Add small hysteresis to each comparator for noise immunity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use two comparators for window detection. Set thresholds with resistor dividers.

categories.name: Interface ICs
categories.description: Robust interface ICs including RS-485/RS-422 transceivers, CAN transceivers, and level shifters for industrial communication.
categories.longDescription: Xinbole's interface IC portfolio provides reliable communication solutions for industrial and automotive applications. The product range includes RS-485/RS-422 transceivers for long-distance differential communication, CAN transceivers for robust automotive and industrial networks, and level shifters for mixed-voltage systems. These interface ICs feature high ESD protection, wide common-mode range, and excellent noise immunity. With support for various data rates and protection levels, Xinbole interface products serve applications from factory automation to automotive networks. The products are designed for reliability in harsh environments with features like thermal shutdown and fault protection. LiTong is your authorized distributor providing selection guidance and technical support for Xinbole interface products.

series.name: XB-CAN Series
series.description: CAN transceivers for automotive and industrial networks

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Interface ICs
selectionGuide.description: Consider communication protocol, data rate, protection requirements, and operating environment

faqs.question: What is the difference between RS-485 and RS-422?
faqs.answer: RS-485 supports multi-point (multi-drop) communication with up to 32 drivers and 32 receivers on the same bus, using half-duplex or full-duplex modes. RS-422 is point-to-point or multi-drop with one driver and up to 10 receivers, typically full-duplex. RS-485 is more common for industrial networks requiring multiple nodes. RS-422 is simpler for point-to-point links. Both use differential signaling for noise immunity. Choose RS-485 for multi-node networks, RS-422 for simpler point-to-point applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use RS-485 for multi-node networks. Use RS-422 for point-to-point links.

faqs.question: What data rates are supported by Xinbole RS-485 transceivers?
faqs.answer: Xinbole RS-485 transceivers support various data rates depending on the model. Standard speed devices (XB-48501) support up to 500kbps for industrial automation. High-speed devices (XB-48520) support up to 20Mbps for fast data acquisition. The maximum reliable distance decreases with higher data rates. At 9600bps, RS-485 can communicate over 1200 meters. At 20Mbps, maximum distance is typically less than 10 meters. Select speed grade based on your data rate and distance requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select speed grade based on data rate. Consider distance vs speed trade-off.

faqs.question: What protection features do Xinbole interface ICs include?
faqs.answer: Xinbole interface ICs include comprehensive protection features: ESD protection (±15kV IEC 61000-4-2 contact/air discharge) for handling electrostatic discharge. Thermal shutdown protects against overheating. Short-circuit protection limits current during bus contention or faults. Common-mode transient immunity ensures reliable operation in noisy environments. Some devices include fail-safe features to ensure defined output states when inputs are open or shorted. These protections ensure reliable operation in harsh industrial environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: All Xinbole interface ICs include ESD and fault protection. Check specific models for additional features.

faqs.question: When should I use CAN bus instead of RS-485?
faqs.answer: Use CAN bus for applications requiring high reliability, collision detection, and priority-based messaging. CAN includes hardware error detection, automatic retransmission, and message prioritization. It's the standard for automotive and many industrial control networks. Use RS-485 for simpler master-slave or peer-to-peer communication where protocol handling is done in software. RS-485 is lower cost and simpler to implement for basic data links. CAN requires more complex protocol stacks but provides more robust communication.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use CAN for robust multi-node networks with priority. Use RS-485 for simpler communication.

faqs.question: How do I terminate an RS-485 bus properly?
faqs.answer: RS-485 buses require termination resistors at both ends to prevent signal reflections. Use 120Ω resistors matching the cable characteristic impedance. Place one resistor at each physical end of the bus, not at every node. For short buses (<10 meters) at low speeds, termination may be optional. For long buses or high speeds, termination is essential. Also use a bias resistor network (typically 560Ω pull-up to 5V and 560Ω pull-down to ground) at one end to ensure idle bus state.
faqs.decisionGuide: Terminate both ends with 120Ω. Add bias resistors for fail-safe idle state.

products.name: RS-485/RS-422 Transceiver
products.shortDescription: Robust RS-485/RS-422 transceiver with high ESD protection and wide common-mode range for industrial applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The XB-48501 is a robust RS-485/RS-422 transceiver designed for demanding industrial environments.
It features high ESD protection and wide common-mode voltage range for reliable communication.
The half-duplex operation and low power consumption make it ideal for multi-node networks.

faeReview.author: Robert Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Communication
faeReview.content: The XB-48501 is a reliable workhorse for industrial RS-485 networks. The ±15kV ESD protection handles harsh factory environments without external protection components. I particularly appreciate the wide common-mode range which maintains communication even with significant ground potential differences between nodes. The fail-safe receiver ensures defined output when lines are idle or open - no false triggers. For industrial automation, building control, or any multi-node RS-485 application, this transceiver delivers robust performance at a competitive price.

alternativeParts.reason: XB-48501 offers wider supply range, higher ESD, and more nodes at lower cost
alternativeParts.useCase: XB-48501 for new designs; MAX485 for legacy compatibility

alternativeParts.reason: XB-48501 provides modern protection and 3.3V support at competitive cost
alternativeParts.useCase: XB-48501 for all new industrial designs

companionParts.description: MCU with UART for communication control

faqs.question: Can XB-48501 operate on 3.3V supply?
faqs.answer: Yes, XB-48501 supports both 3.3V and 5V supply voltages. The logic I/O levels match the supply voltage, making it compatible with 3.3V or 5V microcontrollers. The differential output levels meet RS-485 specifications regardless of supply voltage. This dual-voltage support simplifies mixed-voltage system design and allows use with modern 3.3V microcontrollers without level shifting.
faqs.decisionGuide: XB-48501 works with 3.3V or 5V systems. Select supply based on MCU voltage.

faqs.question: How many devices can be connected to an RS-485 bus?
faqs.answer: RS-485 supports up to 32 unit loads on a single bus. The XB-48501 is a 1/8 unit load device, allowing up to 256 devices on the same bus. This high node count is achieved through high-impedance receiver inputs that draw minimal current from the bus. For maximum node count, ensure all devices are fractional unit load. Standard (1 unit load) devices limit the bus to 32 nodes. Plan your network topology and node count accordingly.
faqs.decisionGuide: XB-48501 supports up to 256 nodes. Check unit load ratings for mixed networks.

faqs.question: What cable should I use for RS-485 networks?
faqs.answer: Use twisted-pair cable for RS-485 to maximize noise immunity. Shielded twisted pair (STP) provides best performance in noisy environments. Characteristic impedance should be 120Ω to match termination resistors. Common choices include CAT5/CAT6 Ethernet cable (100Ω) or dedicated RS-485 cable (120Ω). For long distances or high speeds, use cable with lower capacitance. Avoid running RS-485 cables parallel to high-voltage power cables. Use proper grounding practices for shielded cables.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 120Ω twisted pair cable. Shield for noisy environments.

faqs.question: Does XB-48501 require external ESD protection?
faqs.answer: No, XB-48501 includes integrated ±15kV ESD protection (IEC 61000-4-2) on bus pins, sufficient for most industrial applications. The integrated protection eliminates need for external TVS diodes in typical environments. For extremely harsh environments with frequent severe transients, additional external protection may be considered. The integrated protection saves PCB space and BOM cost while providing reliable ESD immunity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Integrated protection sufficient for most applications. No external components needed.

faqs.question: What is fail-safe operation in RS-485?
faqs.answer: Fail-safe ensures the receiver output is in a defined state (typically high) when the bus is idle, open, or shorted. Without fail-safe, noise on an idle bus can cause random output transitions. XB-48501 includes internal fail-safe biasing that eliminates need for external bias resistors in most applications. The receiver thresholds are offset to ensure logic high output when differential voltage is zero or slightly negative. This simplifies network design and improves reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: XB-48501 internal fail-safe eliminates external bias resistors. Receiver output defined for all bus conditions.

products.name: CAN Bus Transceiver
products.shortDescription: High-speed CAN transceiver with automotive-grade protection and low-power modes for vehicle and industrial networks.
descriptionParagraphs: The XB-CAN01 is a high-speed CAN transceiver designed for automotive and industrial CAN networks.
It supports data rates up to 1Mbps with excellent EMC performance and robust protection features.
The low-power standby mode and remote wake-up capability make it ideal for power-sensitive applications.

faeReview.author: Robert Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Communication
faeReview.content: The XB-CAN01 is a robust CAN transceiver suitable for automotive and industrial applications. The ±58V fault protection handles wiring faults and transients that would damage lesser transceivers. I find the low standby current particularly valuable for automotive battery-powered systems. The AEC-Q100 qualification provides confidence for automotive designs. EMC performance is excellent - this transceiver passes automotive OEM requirements without additional filtering. For any CAN application requiring reliability, this transceiver is an excellent choice.

alternativeParts.reason: XB-CAN01 offers comparable protection at lower cost for standard CAN applications
alternativeParts.useCase: XB-CAN01 for standard CAN; TJA1051 for CAN FD applications

alternativeParts.reason: XB-CAN01 provides superior fault protection for demanding environments
alternativeParts.useCase: XB-CAN01 for harsh environments; SN65HVD230 for basic applications

faqs.question: What is the difference between CAN 2.0 and CAN FD?
faqs.answer: CAN 2.0 (Classical CAN) supports data rates up to 1Mbps with 8 bytes data per frame. CAN FD (Flexible Data-rate) increases data rate up to 8Mbps during data phase and supports up to 64 bytes data per frame. XB-CAN01 supports CAN 2.0 at up to 1Mbps. For CAN FD applications, select a CAN FD compatible transceiver. Both use the same physical layer signaling, but CAN FD requires protocol controller support in the MCU.
faqs.decisionGuide: XB-CAN01 for standard CAN 2.0. Select CAN FD transceiver for higher data rates.

faqs.question: How do I terminate a CAN bus?
faqs.answer: CAN bus requires 120Ω termination resistors at both ends of the bus. Use two 120Ω resistors in parallel with the CANH and CANL lines at each physical end. Do not place termination at intermediate nodes. For short networks (<10 meters), a single 120Ω resistor may suffice. For long networks or high speeds, proper termination at both ends is essential. Some transceivers include internal switchable termination for flexibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Terminate both ends with 120Ω. Do not terminate intermediate nodes.

faqs.question: What is dominant timeout protection?
faqs.answer: Dominant timeout protection prevents permanent bus lockup if a transmitter holds the bus dominant (logic 0) due to hardware or software fault. If the TXD pin remains low longer than the timeout period (typically 1-3ms), the transceiver disables the driver, releasing the bus. This protects the network from a single faulty node. The XB-CAN01 includes this protection, making it suitable for safety-critical applications where bus availability is essential.
faqs.decisionGuide: Dominant timeout protects against bus lockup. Essential for safety-critical networks.

faqs.question: Can XB-CAN01 operate in industrial temperature range?
faqs.answer: Yes, XB-CAN01 is AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualified, supporting -40°C to +125°C ambient temperature. This industrial/automotive grade ensures reliable operation in harsh environments. The extended temperature range makes it suitable for automotive engine compartments, industrial control cabinets, and outdoor applications. The device specifications are guaranteed across the full temperature range, ensuring consistent performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: XB-CAN01 suitable for -40°C to +125°C. Ideal for automotive and industrial applications.

faqs.question: How does remote wake-up work on XB-CAN01?
faqs.answer: Remote wake-up allows the CAN transceiver to detect bus activity while in low-power standby mode and signal the microcontroller to wake up. In standby mode, XB-CAN01 monitors the bus for dominant states. When valid wake-up patterns are detected, the transceiver drives the RXD pin low to wake the MCU. This enables power-saving sleep modes while maintaining network responsiveness. The MCU can then exit standby and resume normal operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standby mode for power saving. Remote wake-up maintains network connectivity.
